focus_point: In ArkUI ArkTS, does animating the radius of a Circle (or a shape) that is passed to the universal attribute .clip() actually re-evaluate/re-render the clip mask per animation frame when the radius is driven by a @State number mutated inside animateTo({duration})? Is .clip(Circle) geometry animatable, or is there a forbidden/non-animatable gotcha requiring a different attribute (e.g. scale/translate on the clipped node, or clipShape via bindTo)? Give the minimal valid pattern for a circular reveal where an overlay Image clip shrinks from maxDistance to 0.
task_excerpt: Circular reveal theme toggle: overlay Image of old snapshot is clipped by a Circle whose radius animates from maxCornerDistance to 0 inside animateTo({duration:500}); the new theme underneath is revealed as the old-snapshot overlay's circular mask shrinks. Need to know if .clip(Circle) radius animates per frame or if .scale on the overlay is the correct animatable primitive.
project_evidence: ArkUI animateTo, .clip, Image, Circle available. No existing clip-animation usage in the project to copy.
platform_surfaces: ArkUI universal attribute .clip(value: Circle | Rect | boolean | CustomBuilder)
ArkUI animateTo({duration, curve, onFinish}, () => state mutation)
ArkUI Circle({width, height}) / CircleShape geometry animation
extra_constraints: State explicitly whether .clip(Circle) re-evaluates per frame when the Circle radius/size is a @State mutated inside animateTo, or whether clip is non-animatable.
If clip is non-animatable, give the recommended animatable alternative (e.g. animate .scale on the overlay Image, or animate the Circle width/height) with exact attribute names.
Flag any forbidden/invalid usage that fails to animate or fails to compile.
Keep it local to the overlay Image clip pattern; do not redesign the architecture.
